name: Rafiul Hasan
bio: Met in first year at BUET. We used to pull all-nighters together before every exam. He moved to Dhaka after graduation but we still try to catch up over biriyani whenever I visit.

name: Nadia Sultana
bio: My closest cousin from Agrabad. We grew up playing in the same neighborhood. She is now working at a bank and we meet every Eid without fail. Video calls every week keep us connected.

name: Tanvir Ahmed
bio: Senior developer I met at a local tech meetup in Chittagong. He mentored me through my first freelance project and taught me more about clean code than any tutorial ever did. Terrible at replying to messages.

name: Priya Chakraborty
bio: We met in a competitive programming club. She now works at a tech startup in Kolkata. We still solve LeetCode problems together on weekends over a voice call — our weird but consistent tradition.

name: Jahangir Alam
bio: My oldest friend from Raozan. We used to fish at the pond behind his house every summer vacation as kids. Life took us in different directions — he is now a schoolteacher in Comilla — but the bond never fades.

name: Samira Khanam
bio: We built our final year web project together and somehow survived three weeks of zero sleep and constant merge conflicts. Now she works remotely for a Canadian agency. She is living proof that hard work pays off.

name: Mehedi Hassan
bio: Classmate who sits two benches ahead in DSA class. We bond over late-night gaming sessions and debating which sorting algorithm is actually the most elegant. He has strong opinions about merge sort.

name: Ayesha Rahman
bio: My elder sister who moved to Dhaka three years ago for her master's degree and stayed for a job at a pharmaceutical company. She sends me care packages and unsolicited life advice in equal measure.
